subject: HSC Mathematics Advanced
description: Past paper and representative practice question bank. Questions are structured in authentic HSC format with full marking guidelines.
note: Questions marked 'representative' are modelled on authentic HSC question styles and difficulty. Verify NESA-sourced questions against official exam papers before production use.

2023-MA-Q1.stem: Which of the following is the domain of the function f(x) = √(3 - x)?

solution.reasoning: For f(x) = √(3 - x) to be defined, the expression under the square root must be non-negative: 3 - x ≥ 0, so x ≤ 3.

criteria.description: Selects B

commonErrors: Choosing A (strict inequality, forgetting x = 3 gives √0 = 0 which is valid)
Reversing the inequality direction

2023-MA-Q2.stem: What is the exact value of sin(210°)?

solution.reasoning: 210° is in the third quadrant (180° + 30°). In Q3, sin is negative. The reference angle is 30°, so sin(30°) = 1/2. Therefore sin(210°) = -1/2.

criteria.description: Selects B

commonErrors: Forgetting the negative sign in Q3
Using the wrong reference angle

2023-MA-Q3.stem: What is the gradient of the tangent to y = x³ - 2x at the point where x = 2?

criteria.description: Selects C

commonErrors: Substituting into y instead of dy/dx
Differentiating incorrectly: forgetting constant -2 differentiates to 0 not -2x

2023-MA-Q4.stem: Which expression is equal to log₂(32) - log₂(4)?

solution.reasoning: Using the quotient law: log₂(32) - log₂(4) = log₂(32/4) = log₂(8) = 3, since 2³ = 8.

criteria.description: Selects B

commonErrors: Dividing: log₂(32)/log₂(4) = 5/2 (incorrect — log laws don't work this way)
Computing 32 - 4 = 28 inside the log

solution.reasoning: ∫2x dx = x² and ∫e^x dx = e^x. Combined: x² + e^x + C. The constant of integration is required.

criteria.description: Selects C

commonErrors: Forgetting +C (option B)
Integrating 2x as 2x² instead of x²

steps: Set each factor to zero: 2x + 1 = 0 or x - 3 = 0
solution.alternativeMethod: Using quadratic formula: x = (5 ± √(25 + 24))/4 = (5 ± 7)/4, giving x = 3 or x = -1/2

criteria.description: Obtains both correct solutions x = -1/2 and x = 3
Substantial progress: correct factorisation or correct substitution into quadratic formula

commonErrors: Factorising as (2x - 1)(x + 3) — sign errors
Using discriminant incorrectly: b² - 4ac = 25 - 4(2)(-3) = 25 + 24 = 49 (note negative c changes sign)

2023-MA-Q11b.stem: Find the equation of the tangent to the curve y = x² + 3x at the point where x = 1.

steps: Find y-coordinate: y = 1² + 3(1) = 4, so point is (1, 4)

criteria.description: Correct equation of tangent y = 5x - 1
Correct gradient m = 5 found, OR correct point (1, 4) identified

commonErrors: Finding y-coordinate by substituting into dy/dx instead of y = f(x)
Using normal gradient (-1/5) instead of tangent gradient
Arithmetic error in expanding point-slope form

2023-MA-Q12.stem: In triangle ABC, AB = 8 cm, BC = 11 cm and angle BAC = 48°. Find the length of AC, correct to one decimal place.

steps: Using the sine rule: BC/sin(BAC) = AC/sin(ABC)... wait — actually use cosine rule since we have two sides and included angle: No — we have AB, BC, angle BAC which is not the included angle.
C = sin⁻¹(0.5404) = 32.7° (check for ambiguous case: C could also be 180° - 32.7° = 147.3°, but A + C = 48° + 147.3° > 180°, so not valid)

criteria.description: Correct answer AC ≈ 14.6 cm with appropriate working
Correct use of sine rule and finds angle B, but arithmetic error in final step
Correct sine rule set up with appropriate values substituted

commonErrors: Applying cosine rule when sine rule is more direct
Not checking for the ambiguous case
Rounding intermediate values and accumulating error

2023-MA-Q13.stem:
For the function f(x) = x³ - 3x² - 9x + 2:
(a) Find the stationary points and determine their nature.
(b) Find any points of inflection.

steps: Stationary points: f'(x) = 0 → x = 3 or x = -1
f''(x) changes sign (negative to positive) at x = 1 → point of inflection
solution.finalAnswer: Local max at (-1, 7), local min at (3, -25), inflection point at (1, -9)

criteria.description: All three points correctly found and classified with y-coordinates
Stationary points correctly found and classified, OR inflection point correct
f'(x) correctly differentiated and set to zero

commonErrors: Forgetting to find y-coordinates of stationary points
Stating x = 1 is an inflection without verifying sign change of f''(x)
Reversing nature: f''(x) < 0 is maximum (concave down) not minimum

2023-MA-Q14.stem: Find the area of the region enclosed by the curve y = x² - 4 and the x-axis.

steps: Determine sign: for -2 < x < 2, y = x² - 4 < 0 (curve is below x-axis)
Area = -∫[-2 to 2] (x² - 4) dx (negative because curve is below x-axis)

criteria.description: Correct answer 32/3 with full working shown
Correct integral set up and evaluated, but arithmetic error in final simplification
Correct limits identified and integral set up (even if not evaluated correctly)
Finds x-intercepts at x = ±2

commonErrors: Not taking the negative (area below x-axis is ∫|f(x)|dx, not ∫f(x)dx which would give -32/3)
Limits: using 0 to 2 only (forgetting the negative part)
Antiderivative errors: x³/3 becoming x²/2

2023-MA-Q15.stem: A farmer wants to fence a rectangular paddock using an existing straight fence as one side. She has 120 m of fencing. Find the maximum area of the paddock and the dimensions that achieve this.

steps: Let the width (perpendicular to existing fence) be x metres. The length (parallel to existing fence) is (120 - 2x) metres.
solution.finalAnswer: Maximum area = 1800 m², dimensions 30 m × 60 m

criteria.description: Correct maximum area of 1800 m² with dimensions 30 m × 60 m, and verification of maximum
Correct x = 30 found and area calculated, but maximum not verified
Correct expression for A in terms of x, differentiated correctly
Correct diagram or expression showing constraint 2x + length = 120

commonErrors: Using three sides of x each (forgetting one side is the existing fence)
Not verifying that the stationary point is a maximum
Finding x = 30 but not stating the length = 60
Not including units in the final answer

2023-MA-Q16.stem:
A population of bacteria grows according to the model P = 500e^(kt). Initially there are 500 bacteria. After 2 hours there are 2000 bacteria.
(a) Find the value of k, giving an exact answer.
(b) Find how long it takes for the population to reach 10 000, correct to one decimal place.

criteria.description: (a) Correct exact value k = ln2 (or equivalent ln4/2)
(b) Correct answer t ≈ 4.3 hours with appropriate working
(b) Correct equation 10000 = 500e^(kt) set up and simplified to 20 = e^(kt)

commonErrors: (a) Writing k = ln4/2 without simplifying to ln2
(b) Using approximate decimal value of k instead of exact ln2
Not solving for t after taking logs

2023-MA-Q17.stem:
The function f(x) = 3sin(2x) + 1 is defined for 0 ≤ x ≤ 2π.
(a) State the amplitude and period of f(x).
(b) Find all solutions of f(x) = 1 in the given domain.

steps: u = 0, π, 2π, 3π, 4π (for 0 ≤ x ≤ 2π, we need 0 ≤ u ≤ 4π)
solution.finalAnswer: Amplitude = 3, Period = π; x = 0, π/2, π, 3π/2, 2π

criteria.description: (a) Amplitude = 3 AND Period = π — both required for the mark
(b) All five correct solutions listed
(b) At least two correct solutions found using correct method

commonErrors: (a) Period = 2 (forgetting to divide 2π by b = 2)
(b) Only finding x = 0 and x = π (not extending to full domain)
(b) Forgetting the endpoints x = 0 and x = 2π
(b) Dividing by 3 before solving but not dividing the right-hand side

2023-MA-Q18.stem:
The heights of students at a school are normally distributed with a mean of 165 cm and a standard deviation of 8 cm.
(a) A student is selected at random. Find the probability that the student's height is between 157 cm and 181 cm.
(b) Using the empirical rule, what percentage of students have a height between 149 cm and 181 cm?

steps: By empirical rule: 95% of data lies within 2σ of the mean

criteria.description: (a) Correct probability 0.8185 with z-scores and method shown
(a) Correct z-scores z = -1 and z = 2 found
(b) Correct identification of ±2σ and answer 95%

commonErrors: (b) Saying 68% (confusing with ±1σ)
(a) Not subtracting: just reading P(Z < 2) = 0.9772 as the answer

2023-MA-Q19.stem:
Differentiate the following with respect to x, simplifying where possible:
(a) y = x²e^x
(b) y = sin(x) / (x² + 1)

steps: (a) Product rule: u = x², v = e^x; u' = 2x, v' = e^x
dy/dx = u'v + uv' = 2xe^x + x²e^x = xe^x(2 + x)
(b) Quotient rule: u = sinx, v = x² + 1; u' = cosx, v' = 2x

criteria.description: (a) Correct derivative xe^x(x+2) or equivalent unsimplified form
(a) Correct product rule applied: 2xe^x + x²e^x (unsimplified)
(b) Correct derivative with quotient rule applied correctly
(b) Correct identification of u, v, u', v' and quotient rule structure set up

commonErrors: (a) Multiplying derivatives: (2x)(e^x) without the uv' term
(b) Getting numerator sign wrong: should be cosx(x²+1) - sinx(2x), not plus
(b) Squaring the entire fraction instead of just the denominator

2023-MA-Q20.stem:
The curves y = x² and y = 6x - x² intersect at two points.
(a) Find the x-coordinates of the points of intersection.
(b) Find the area of the region enclosed between the two curves.

steps: (b) Determine which curve is on top: at x = 1, y = x² gives 1; y = 6x - x² gives 5
So y = 6x - x² is above y = x² for 0 < x < 3
solution.finalAnswer: (a) x = 0 and x = 3  (b) Area = 9 square units

criteria.description: (a) Both intersection points x = 0 and x = 3 correctly found
(a) Correct equation 2x² - 6x = 0 or x² = 6x - x² set up
(b) Correct area of 9 square units with integration shown
(b) Correct integral ∫(6x - 2x²)dx set up with correct limits
(b) Identifies which curve is on top and subtracts correctly

commonErrors: Subtracting in wrong order (area would be negative — take absolute value as a check)
Using incorrect limits of integration
Antiderivative error: integrating x² as x³/2 instead of x³/3

2023-MA-Q21.stem:
Emma takes out a home loan of $400 000 at an interest rate of 4.8% per annum, compounded monthly. She makes monthly repayments of M dollars over 25 years.
(a) Show that the monthly interest rate is 0.4%.
(b) Using the present value annuity formula PV = M[1-(1+r)^(-n)]/r, find the monthly repayment M, correct to the nearest cent.

criteria.description: (a) Shows 4.8/12 = 0.4 with clear working
(b) Correct M ≈ $2292.58 with full substitution shown
(b) Correct formula substituted with r = 0.004, n = 300
(b) Correct values r = 0.004 and n = 300 identified

commonErrors: n = 25 (using years instead of months — must be 300)
r = 0.048 (using annual rate instead of monthly 0.004)
Using future value formula instead of present value formula

2023-MA-Q22.stem:
A discrete random variable X has the following probability distribution:

| x    | 1    | 2    | 3    | 4    |
|------|------|------|------|------|
| P(X=x) | 0.1  | 0.3  | k    | 0.2  |

(a) Find the value of k.
(b) Calculate E(X).
(c) Calculate Var(X).

steps: (a) All probabilities sum to 1: 0.1 + 0.3 + k + 0.2 = 1 → k = 0.4

criteria.description: (c) Var(X) = 0.81 with E(X²) = 8.1 shown
(c) Correct E(X²) = 8.1 calculated

commonErrors: (c) Computing [E(X)]² = E(X²): Var(X) = E(X²) - [E(X)]², must subtract squared expectation
(b) Adding x values instead of x·P(X=x)
(a) Setting k = 1 - 0.3 = 0.7 (forgetting the other probabilities)

meta.buildNote: This initial bank covers representative questions modelled on authentic HSC Maths Advanced exam style. Expand with actual NESA past paper questions (2020–2025) sourced directly from NESA publications. Each NESA question requires verification of exact wording, options, and official marking criteria.
